title: Dimensional Rift Shader Portal
description: A virtual city floats in space, fractured by a glowing dimensional rift. The user peers through the rift to see a spaceship hallway on the other side, manipulating custom shaders that control the rift's distortion, chromatic aberration, edge glow, dissolve pattern, and color grading across both worlds.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that loads VirtualCity.glb as the primary scene and space_ship_hallway.glb visible through a shader-driven dimensional rift portal. Camera setup: perspective camera at (0, 15, 40) looking at the city center with OrbitControls limited to a 60-degree vertical arc. Lighting: the city side uses a warm directional light (0xFFCC88) plus blue ambient (0x223355); the hallway side uses cold fluorescent tones (0x88CCFF). Core shader implementation: create a custom ShaderMaterial rift plane (roughly 8x12 units) floating vertically above the city. The rift shader uses a render texture (WebGLRenderTarget) of the hallway scene as its base, then applies layered distortion effects. Render the hallway to an offscreen target each frame and pass it as a uniform. Interactions (7 steps with validation): 1) A slider 'Rift Distortion' (0.0-2.0, default 0.5) controls sine-wave UV displacement amplitude in the fragment shader — validate range. 2) A slider 'Chromatic Shift' (0.0-0.05, default 0.01) offsets R/G/B channels separately when sampling the hallway texture — validate range. 3) A slider 'Edge Glow Intensity' (0.0-5.0, default 2.0) controls an additive glow (cyan/magenta) at the rift border using a Fresnel-like falloff based on UV distance from center — validate range. 4) A slider 'Dissolve Threshold' (0.0-1.0, default 0.3) uses a Perlin noise function in the shader to dissolve/reveal portions of the rift — validate range. 5) A color picker 'City Tint' applies a post-process color grading uniform to all city materials via onBeforeCompile injection — validate hex format. 6) A slider 'Scanline Density' (0-200, integer, default 50) adds horizontal scanlines to the hallway render texture in the rift shader — validate integer. 7) A toggle 'Animate Rift' enables time-based animation that oscillates distortion and dissolve threshold sinusoidally — validate boolean. Use a dat.GUI panel for all controls. The rift plane should gently hover with a subtle vertex shader oscillation. Ensure both GLB models are visible simultaneously and the hallway is only seen through the rift portal effect.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  riftDistortion: number,         // amplitude of sine-wave UV displacement in rift shader (0.0-2.0)
  chromaticShift: number,         // RGB channel separation offset for chromatic aberration (0.0-0.05)
  edgeGlowIntensity: number,         // additive glow intensity at rift edges (0.0-5.0)
  dissolveThreshold: number,         // cutoff value for dissolve pattern effect (0.0-1.0)
  colorGradingStrength: number,         // blend factor for color grading between worlds (0.0-1.0)
  cityLightIntensity: number,         // multiplier for warm directional light on city side (0.0-3.0)
  hallwayLightIntensity: number,         // multiplier for cold fluorescent light on hallway side (0.0-3.0)
  riftRotationSpeed: number,         // angular velocity of rift plane rotation in radians per second
  renderTargetUpdated: boolean,         // whether hallway render target has been updated this frame
  shaderUniformsValid: boolean,         // whether all shader uniforms are within valid ranges
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance from camera to city center in world units
  totalInteractionCount: number,         // cumulative count of all slider interactions performed
}
